manual_audit_notes.llm_analysis_excerpt: The City of San Jose Resolution establishes Commercial Linkage Fees for various non-residential development categories based on gross square footage. The fees act as a direct cost on developers, ranging from $0.00 to $18.706.00 per square foot depending on use type and size, with credits available for demolished non-residential space. The provided text does not contain evidence or claims regarding household cost-of-living impacts or indirect cost pass-through.

fail_closed_reasons: analysis_sufficiency:Sufficient for fee rates and direct developer costs; insufficient for household impact analysis (fail closed).

key_points: Fee Rates: Office (<100,000 sq. ft.) $3.00; Retail (≥100,000 sq. ft.) $3.00; Retail (<100,000 sq. ft.) $0.00; Hotel $5.00; Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq. ft.) $3.00; Industrial/Research and Development (<100,000 sq. ft.) $0.00; Warehouse $5.00; Residential Care $18.706.00.
Affected Categories: Non-Residential Projects including Office, Retail, Hotel, Industrial/Research and Development, Warehouse, and Residential Care.
Direct Developer Cost Evidence: The resolution explicitly states 'All Non-Residential Projects... shall pay the following,' confirming a direct cost obligation for developers.
Credits: A per-square-foot credit is applied for legally permitted demolition of existing non-residential buildings, calculated based on the fee of the prior use.
Household Impact: The source text provides no evidence regarding household cost-of-living impacts or indirect pass-through costs.

ranked_candidates.snippet: The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use The production and preservation of affordable housing is a priority for City Council.
ranked_candidates.title: Commercial Linkage Fee | City of San José

ranked_candidates.snippet: Commercial Linkage Fee in San Jose On Tuesday, September 1 st, 2020, the San Jose City Council approved a Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Ordinance, joining all of the other major cities in the South Bay that have already adopted fees. CLFs require commercial developers to pay a fee to address the affordable housing needs created by future employees.
ranked_candidates.title: Commercial Linkage Fees - SV@Home

ranked_candidates.snippet: The commercial linkage fee program, adopted in 2020, requires developers of office, retail, hotel, industrial, research and development, warehouse and residential care spaces to pay $12-15 per square foot, unless they promise to build the equivalent in affordable housing.
ranked_candidates.title: UPDATE: San Jose gives more fee breaks to commercial developers

structured_policy_facts.source_excerpt: SAN JOSE, CA - At Tuesday’s San Jose City Council meeting, the pressing need for affordable housing spurred broad consensus on establishing a Commercial Linkage Fee, implementing a new framework for developer fees that will fund affordable housing projects in the City. “I commend my council colleagues, community organizations, and business partners for working together to find middle ground on a solution that will generate millions of dollars annually for affordable housing, while ensuring that job-creating projects can move forward,” **said San Jose Mayor Sam Liccardo.** “As we confront the d
structured_policy_facts.source_title: San Jose City Council Passes Commercial Linkage Fee | News | City of San José

structured_policy_facts.source_excerpt: The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing. The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use. The San José City Council voted on December 11, 2018 to pursue a nexus and a feasibility study on a potential commercial linkage fee in San José. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reducti
structured_policy_facts.source_title: Commercial Linkage Fee | City of San José

top_results.snippet: SAN JOSE, CA - At Tuesday’s San Jose City Council meeting, the pressing need for affordable housing spurred broad consensus on establishing a Commercial Linkage Fee, implementing a new framework for developer fees that will fund affordable housing projects in the City. “I commend my council colleagues, community organizations, and business partners for working together to find middle ground on a solution that will generate millions of dollars annually for affordable housing, while ensuring that job-creating projects can move forward,” **said San Jose Mayor Sam Liccardo.** “As we confront the deepest economic crisis of our lifetimes, and our persistent affordable housing crisis, we need to follow a balanced course that enables more families both to put food on the table and to stay housed.". Under the approved City Council plan, Downtown office developers with projects more than 100,000 square feet will have three fee options:. For office projects less than 100,000 square feet citywide, City Council amended that the first 50,000 square feet will not be charged the $3 per square foot fee.
top_results.title: San Jose City Council Passes Commercial Linkage Fee | News | City of San José

top_results.snippet: The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing. The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use. The San José City Council voted on December 11, 2018 to pursue a nexus and a feasibility study on a potential commercial linkage fee in San José. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply. | Industrial/Research and Development(≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply.
top_results.title: Commercial Linkage Fee | City of San José

top_results.snippet: (b) Adopt a resolution amending the 2021-2022 Schedule of Fees and Charges (Resolution No. 72737, as amended) to add the Commercial Linkage Fee and make certain changes thereto superseding Resolution No. 79705 to:   (1) Modify options for payment to provide a deferred payment option for Office and Industrial / research and development projects of 100,000 square feet or more including installments after the scheduling of the final building inspection;   (2) Provide a 20% discount to the fee amount when the fee is paid full at or prior to building permit issuance;   (3) Add the credit for constructing or restricting new affordable housing units; and   (4) Increase the maximum square footage for exemption of non-residential office buildings to 50,000 square feet.
top_results.title: City of San José - File #: 22-269
